pr.title: PR #122 — Global Capability Registry Framework v1
pr.description:
## Summary

Adds the **canonical identity layer** for Program 1. GitHub PR numbers are repository-local; the Global Capability ID/number becomes the primary identity so capability history stays continuous across repositories, workers, and future systems. Repository PR numbers become *references*, not identity.

Example mapping the registry encodes:
```
plamen-hristov/Axiom-platform PR #111  -> Global Capability #111
plamen-dev/Axiom-platform     PR #1    -> Global Capability #112
plamen-dev/Axiom-platform     PR #10   -> Global Capability #121
```

New module `src/axiom_core/global_capability_registry.py`:
- `GlobalCapabilityStatus` — `PROPOSED, OPEN, MERGED, CLOSED, SUPERSEDED`.
- `GlobalCapabilityRepositoryRef` — owner/name/PR number/PR url/branch/commit_sha/merge_sha (`from_dict`/`to_dict`).
- `GlobalCapabilityWorkerRef` — `worker_id, worker_type`.
- `GlobalCapabilityValidationSummary` — `new_tests, total_tests, skipped_tests, ruff_clean, ci_status, notes`.
- `GlobalCapabilityEntry` — the full record: `global_capability_id/number, capability_name, worker, repository, validation, primary_program, secondary_programs, parent_capability_ids, related_capability_ids, affected_files, status, created_at/updated_at, schema_version, raw_metadata`.
- `GlobalCapabilityRegistry` — ordered de-duplicated collection.
- `GlobalCapabilityReport` — `report_id, entry_count, status_counts, program_counts, schema_version, entries`.
- `GlobalCapabilityEvidence` + `GlobalCapabilityRegistryEngine` (`create / get_report / list_reports / export_report`).

**Authoritative global ordering** (`_sort_key`):
```python
(global_capability_number, created_at,
 repository_owner, repository_name,
 repository_pr_number, global_capability_id)
```

**Duplicate global numbers rejected** at create time (`ValueError`). **Raw metadata preserved** verbatim. **Schema versioning** via `SCHEMA_VERSION = "1.0"` (overridable per entry).

CLI (registered in `command_registry.py`, `test_selection_engine.py`, `test_command_registry.py`):
- `axiom global-capability-create [--registry-file <rf>] [--json-output]`
- `axiom global-capability-list [--json-output]`
- `axiom global-capability-show <report_id> [--json-output]`
- `axiom global-capability-export <report_id> [--format markdown|json|csv]`

**Three export formats**: JSON, Markdown timeline (`#112 Name [MERGED] (owner/repo PR #1)`), and CSV (flattened columns). Evidence bundle under `artifacts/global_capability_registry/<run_id>/`: `global_capability_request.json`, `global_capability_result.json`, `global_capability_summary.md`, `global_capability_timeline.csv`, `pass_fail.json` (+ `report.json`).

## What changed / didn't
- **Changed behavior**: new additive identity registry + 4 CLI commands. No existing framework modified.
- **Did not change**: Revit 2024 baseline, architecture/doctrine, dependencies. No GitHub API, no automation, no orchestration, no event timeline.
- **Strategic fit**: identity/registry layer — capability metadata belongs in registries; this makes capability history continuous and machine-traceable across repos/workers.

## Validation
- `poetry run pytest -q` → **3928 passed, 1 skipped** (+46 new; PR #121 baseline 3882).
- `poetry run ruff check src/ tests/` → **0 errors**.
- CLI create/list/show/export verified incl. all 3 export formats and error paths (not-found → exit 2, path-traversal → exit 1, invalid format → exit 1); deterministic ordering confirmed (112 before 121); duplicate-number rejection verified.
- Revit live validation: not required (no adapter/baseline changes).


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev
